senses.definition: A French dish of white beans cooked slowly with meat and herbs.
examples: We had cassoulet for dinner at the bistro.
The cassoulet was rich, warm, and filling.

senses.definition: Plural of cassoulet: French dishes of white beans cooked slowly with meat and herbs.
examples: The menu listed two cassoulets.
They ordered different cassoulets for the table.

senses.definition: Plural of cassowary: large flightless birds from Australia and New Guinea.
examples: Cassowaries can run very fast.
Zoo visitors were warned to stay away from the cassowaries.

senses.definition: A large flightless bird from Australia and New Guinea, with strong legs and a horn-like crest on its head.
examples: A cassowary walked through the forest.
Cassowaries are known for their powerful kicks.

senses.definition: A South African armoured vehicle used to carry troops and protect them from mines.
examples: The soldiers travelled in a casspir.
A casspir is designed to resist landmines.

senses.definition: Plural of casspir: South African armoured vehicles used to carry troops and protect them from mines.
examples: Several casspirs were parked outside the base.
The convoy included two casspirs.

senses.definition: A pungent aromatic ginger-like root used in traditional medicine and cooking.
examples: Cassumunar is used in some herbal remedies.
The recipe calls for cassumunar root.

senses.definition: Plural of cassumunar: pungent aromatic ginger-like roots used in traditional medicine and cooking.
examples: The shop sold dried cassumunars.
Cassumunars are sometimes used in herbal mixtures.

senses.definition: A rare variant spelling of cassumunar, a pungent aromatic ginger-like root used in traditional medicine and cooking.
examples: The label used the spelling cassumuniar.
This rare form likely refers to cassumunar.

senses.definition: A short form of the female given name Cassandra or Cassie.
examples: Cassy is coming to the meeting.
Her friends call her Cassy.

senses.definition: A parasitic plant genus of twining vine-like species, also called dodder laurel.
examples: Cassytha grows by attaching itself to other plants.
The botanist identified the vine as cassytha.

senses.definition: A plant family that includes cassytha and related species.
examples: Cassythaceae are found in tropical regions.
The classification places cassytha in Cassythaceae.

senses.definition: To throw something so that it moves through the air.
examples: She cast the stone into the river.
He cast the rope across the gap.

senses.definition: To choose actors for a film, play, or show.
examples: They cast her as the lead role.
The director cast the new movie last month.

senses.definition: The actors in a film, play, or show.
examples: The cast did an excellent job.
The film has a strong cast.

senses.definition: A hard protective covering around an injured part of the body.
examples: She wore a cast on her arm.
The doctor removed the cast after six weeks.

senses.definition: The shape or form of something, especially its appearance or style.
examples: The room had a medieval cast.
His voice had a gloomy cast.

senses.definition: Plural of castability, the quality of being able to be cast or made into a cast shape.
examples: The material's castabilities are important in manufacturing.
Different alloys have different castabilities.

senses.definition: The quality of a material that makes it suitable for being cast into a shape.
examples: The metal has good castability.
Engineers tested the castability of the alloy.

senses.definition: Able to be cast into a shape or used in casting.
examples: This alloy is highly castable.
The material is castable at low temperatures.

senses.definition: Able to be chosen for a role or suitable for casting in a production.
examples: She is very castable as a romantic lead.
The actor has a castable screen presence.

senses.definition: An Italian sweet fritter made from dough, often served during Carnival.
examples: We ate castagnole during the festival.
Castagnole are small, sweet, and fried.

senses.definition: A name used in literature and classical tradition for a source of inspiration, especially poetic inspiration.
examples: She drank from Castalia in her poem.
Castalia can symbolize creative inspiration.

senses.definition: Relating to Castalia, or to poetic inspiration.
examples: The poet used Castalian imagery.
Her work has a Castalian tone.

senses.definition: A poetic or classical name for the Muses, the goddesses of the arts.
examples: The poet invoked the Castalides.
The Castalides were associated with song and learning.

senses.definition: A rare classical or literary name, often used in reference to a person or character name.
examples: Castalio appears in older literary works.
The name Castalio has a classical sound.

senses.definition: A name used for a chestnut or chestnut tree in some scientific or classical contexts.
examples: Castana appears in older botanical writing.
The word castana is related to chestnuts.

senses.definition: Relating to chestnuts; chestnut-colored.
examples: The horse had a castane coat.
The old text uses castane to mean chestnut-colored.

senses.definition: A genus of trees that includes chestnuts.
examples: Castanea includes the sweet chestnut.
The botanist studied Castanea species.

senses.definition: Relating to Castanea or chestnuts.
examples: The castanean trees were healthy.
Castanean is a rare botanical adjective.
